programme.title: Jodha Akbar
programme.desc: Jodha Akbar is a period drama set in the sixteenth century, focusing on the political marriage between Mughal Emperor Akbar and Rajput Princess Jodha Bai. Initially a marriage of convenience, it evolves into a deep love story that changes the fate of India. The series portrays the era's wars, the complex relationships between the Mughals and Rajputs, and the political dynamics of the royal court. Akbar, raised as a ruthless conqueror, learns the meaning of love and compassion through Jodha, who despises the Mughals for their invasions. Their relationship transforms Akbar from a heartless ruler into the beloved Emperor Akbar, known for his justice and wisdom.

programme.desc: Zindagi Ki Mehek is an Indian television drama series that aired from 2016 to 2018. The story revolves around Mehek Sharma, a talented cook from a middle-class family, and Shaurya Khanna, a wealthy and arrogant restaurateur. Their paths cross when Mehek participates in a cooking competition that Shaurya is judging. Despite their contrasting personalities and initial conflicts, they fall in love and face numerous challenges together, including family opposition and personal vendettas. The show blends themes of love, family, and ambition with a strong emphasis on culinary arts.

programme.title: Forgotten Treasures Of Egypt
programme.desc: Feast your eyes on these testaments to one of antiquity's greatest civilizations and the men who discovered Tutankhamun.

programme.desc: Ahead of his rematch with Chris Eubank Jr, Liam Smith sits down with Anna Woolhouse to discuss his family's journey through boxing and life away from the ring.

programme.title: Off Limits
programme.desc: Ahead of his rematch with Chris Eubank Jr, Liam Smith sits down with Anna Woolhouse to discuss his family's journey through boxing and life away from the ring.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville sits down with former teammate and opponent Dimitar Berbatov to discuss their time in the game and some of the most notable matches in which they played.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville is joined by one of England's greatest-ever goalscorers, Alan Shearer, to look back on the most memorable matches from his career.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville sits down with Ian Wright to discuss their time in the game and some of the most notable matches in which they played.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville sits down with former teammate Paul Scholes to discuss their time in the game and some of the most notable matches in which they played.

programme.title: Sporting Heroes
programme.desc: Cricketer Steve Harmison interviews former Liverpool player and Newcastle United manager Kevin Keegan about his prolific career. Keegan was twice named European Player of the Year.

programme.title: Sporting Heroes
programme.desc: Gary Newbon interviews Olympic champion swimmer Rebecca Adlington about her glittering career in the sport. Adlington claimed two gold medals at the 2008 games in Beijing.

programme.title: Off Limits
programme.desc: Ahead of her bout with Alycia Baumgardner, Mikaela Mayer sits down with Anna Woolhouse to discuss the challenges of her life before boxing and her journey towards becoming world champion.

programme.title: Monsters and Myths
programme.desc: Monsters have always been part of our nightmares and fairy tales. But where do they actually come from? How much of them is truth, how much is myth? Going to great lengths in this documentary, the filmmakers join scientists to explore a number of the best known sagas and legends in the world.

programme.title: Expedition Wallacea
programme.desc: 150 years ago Alfred Wallace used his observations on Indonesian islands to develop a theory of evolution independently of Darwin's. Now Matthias Kopfmüller's team want to add to Wallace's work: can the undersea evidence explain what Wallace found on land? What Wallace had recognised is that within a single island group there seemed to be a barrier that species could not pass.

programme.title: Urban Jungle
programme.desc: We think cities belong to us, but the concrete jungle's teaming with animals - whether its monkeys in Japan, sparrows in Germany, Turkish rats or even Manatee in Florida. We make friends with some, but most go about their lives unseen. Some people see wild animals as pests, and dangerous to our health, others believe they are crucial to our well-being and belong here as much as we do.

programme.title: Born to Fly
programme.desc: We envy birds, watching them enjoy the freedom of a 3 dimensional space. Flight allows them to get to hard to reach places and preform aerial acrobatics in protecting home ranges and searching for food.

programme.title: Deserts On The Move
programme.desc: Deserts On The Move - Asia: China and India are the world's two most populous countries. They are also burdened with two of the most famous ‘hot' deserts - the Gobi desert in Mongolia-China and the Thar desert in India-Pakistan. Most people do not think of China as a desert nation - and yet it is among the world's largest. A staggering third of China's land area, 2.5 million square kilometres, is useless sand.

programme.title: The Biblical Plagues
programme.desc: The Biblical Plagues - Darkness over Egypt: For a long time, scientists have tried to prove that nine of the Ten Plagues described in the Old Testament point to a serious breakdown of the climate, triggering an ecological chain reaction. Apart from theological considerations, scholars all over the world have studied individual phenomena, trying to explain the causes of this Biblical disaster.

programme.title: How the Vikings Changed the World
programme.desc: A nation of seafarers and merchants, the Vikings revolutionised exploration and trading in the Middle Ages and discovered America 500 years before Columbus. This gripping film unveils their bad reputation as wild, murdering and pillaging norsemen as misguided and reveals a strikingly tolerant people with an elaborate trading network that stretched right through Europe. For 900 years, their light, manoeuvrable sailing-ships could not be matched. As the best seafarers of their time, daring, tolerant explorers and expert merchants, they set new benchmarks and left their mark everywhere.

programme.title: Secrets Of Angkor Wat
programme.desc: Temples, designed to honor the Gods, have been at the heart of cities since earliest times. Often they have been the biggest and most impressive buildings of their age. But mysterious ancient temples crop up across the ancient world. Why are so many of these monuments carefully aligned with the sun?

programme.title: Into the Ravine
programme.desc: Architect and property developer Greg meets up with Riaan to do something most 20-year-olds would consider extreme, canyoning through the Storms River!

programme.title: Red Dunes
programme.desc: After getting a puncture on the unforgiving Klahari terrain, Riaan meats up with 4x4 expert Andre and they head out into the wild Kalahari outback.

programme.title: Handlebars & Bridals
programme.desc: Heading to Cape Town's famous Table Mountain, Riaan and Moyra find out that the fastest way to sightsee this iconic city is by off road scooter.

programme.title: New Heights
programme.desc: Impressed by Archie, who took him and Wanita seal snorkeling, Riaan takes this fish out of the water and teaches him to fly.

programme.title: Down The River
programme.desc: Riaan is joined by beauty therapist Rykie for a two day kayak trip on the Orange River. This iconic river feeds the Kalahari and is an oasis in the South African desert.

programme.title: Leap of Faith
programme.desc: Having met Etienne through Magreet at the surf shop in Jay-bay, Riaan convinces him to swap his surfboard for a bungee cord.

programme.title: Getting a Grip
programme.desc: After some training on the walls at Johannesburg's City Rock, Riaan heads out to Hartebeespoort and the cliffs of Chospile to put his newly learned skills to the test.

programme.title: Kalahari Staple
programme.desc: Hunting isn't for everyone, but in the Kalahari region it is a staple for how most people get their meat. Riaan and Yancke undergo some gun safety training.

programme.title: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: Riaan and his guest Dan Nicol head to northern Johannesburg to see who is the champion of the off-road tracks only to have the day take an unexpected turn.

programme.title: Reliving the Adventure
programme.desc: While going on an adventure of his own Riaan looks back at all his experiences with incredible guests and exciting adventures.

programme.title: Into the Darkness
programme.desc: Riaan convinces his South African friend, Danie, to meet him in Israel for a fantastic surprise adventure.

programme.title: Thicker Than Water
programme.desc: Riaan meets Kayak guide, Ofer, on the northern section of the Dead Sea and they head off to explore sink holes, and underwater chimneys.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The staff prepares for two new woolly monkeys from Switzerland, and the male capuchins are put to the test with a clever puzzle feeder.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: It's graduation day as baby golden-cheeked gibbon Mikado moves in with his new foster mum Alex, full time, and geriatric stumpie Gerald is rushed to the operating theatre.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison travels to Vietnam for a ground-breaking project. Two rescued gibbons are to be released into their natural habitat, but will they have the courage to leave their old lives behind?

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Orang-utan Oshine is introduced to two youngsters, and it's an emotional day when the ugly monkeys, from a research facility in Scotland, go outside for the first time.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Hananya is busy showing everyone who is boss, whilst baby woolly Diego joins his family in the big enclosure, and golden-cheeked gibbon Zoey is pregnant.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: There is bad news for the stump-tailed macaques who have recently arrived at the park from a lab facility in Scotland. Wildlife vet John Lewis pays a special visit to give them a health check, but has to call in the services of a top London dentist when he realises they have serious dental problems.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Jeremy is on a mission to coax overweight Oshine into the great outdoors, but to get to the enclosure the big orang-utan has to navigate a series of tunnels, and she is not keen. So Jeremy has to go in on his hands and knees to guide her through. Meanwhile there is trouble at the stumpie house when the boys finally meet the girls. Sam proves a big hit, but Jonathan gets over excited and has to cool off.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The team are in Lebanon on a rescue mission to pick up a very lonely chimp. Kiki has lived on her own in a cage for the last eight years, but there are problems when it is realised that her cage has rusted closed and the team have to resort to drastic measures to get her out. Once at the airport they have to keep Kiki calm or she won't be allowed to board the flight for the UK.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Chimp Kiki finally arrives at the park after a mammoth journey from the Middle East, but having lived on her own in a cage in Beirut for the last eight years, she is unnerved by the sounds of the other chimps in the room next door. Meanwhile there is great excitement at the golden-cheeked gibbon house with news of Zoey's new arrival in the middle of the night.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: New arrival Kiki meets Patricia. Having lived on her own, Kiki is not used to other chimps so the team are not sure how she will react to others of her own kind. Meanwhile, overweight orang-utan Oshine has a special visitor from South Africa - but will she remember her? Woolly monkey Bueno has to have emergency dental treatment when the veterinary team discover he has several rotten teeth.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Jeremy decides to find out exactly how much weight orang-utan Oshine has lost since she arrived at the park from South Africa - but will he be able to tempt her onto the scales? Chimp Hananya is taken by surprise when he meets Kiki, the latest addition to his group. Kiki is about to undertake a crash course in chimpanzee politics, and baby golden-cheeked gibbon Mikado shows off the latest stages of his colour change.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The nursery chimps get the better of animal manager Jeremy, and there's tension when a baby woolly monkey is born, but is rejected by its mother.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Some scientists believe the survival of mankind hinges upon the revolutionary science of Artificial Intelligence.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: For decades, scientists have been searching for proof that extraterrestrials exist. But just where does alien life exist? And what does it look like?

programme.title: What On Earth
programme.desc: Embark on an eye-opening journey as experts investigate the startling mysteries captured by satellites orbiting the Earth: from hidden worlds to unexplainable natural phenomena.

programme.title: What On Earth

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A series of occurrences taking place over several years in Big Bend National Park, in Texas, are seen by many experts to indicate the presence of extraterrestrial activity.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A series of deaths and disappearances in Death Valley National Park leads to the discovery of forces that are believed to inhabit the park's secret underworld.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A look at America's network of national parks, which represents vast, dense expanses of virtually unexplored wilderness. This programme visits Grand Canyon National Park, where a strange series of events leads researchers to speculate whether its power comes from forces that cannot be explained

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Everglades National Park: A plane crash in the Everglades National Park leads to strange hauntings - and some believe the park may exert a dark, transformative power.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigate released documents regarding alien and UFO encounters, made accessible to the public in 2011 by the Freedom of Information Act. We investigate mass UFO sightings, personal abductions, government cover-ups and breaking alien news from around the world. We delve into the "photoshopping" of space, the value of Wikileaks, and the role social media plays in alien stories.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: It's the ultimate close encounter. There have been thousands of reported cases all over the world.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: UFOs. They can fly at blinding speed or hover effortlessly just a few feet above the ground. And they can do it all without making a sound. How is this possible?

programme.title: Evidence Of The Unexplained
programme.desc: A US navy warship is orbited by UFOs.

programme.desc: Dark Matter and Dark Energy make up 96% of the Universe.

programme.desc: Stunning rings of Saturn have mesmerized countless scientists over the centuries.

programme.title: S1 E11 The Outer Planets
programme.desc: New discoveries are creating a fundamental rethinking of our solar system.

programme.title: S3 E12 Cosmic Phenomena
programme.desc: A variety of cosmic events have both helpful and harmful effects on life on Earth.

programme.title: Colours of Nature
programme.desc: From subtle to shocking, colour enhances the beauty of the natural world. Colour is a language to most living things, whether as a warning, camouflage or a come on. But there's more to colour than just pigment. Colours define how many animals behave, how they mate and how they inter-act with one another. For us the beautiful and varied shades of the animal kingdom form a spec-tacular kaleidoscope of colours. With all the diversity in the world, sit back and enjoy all the col-ours of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Seasons Of The Otter
programme.desc: Most of the life of an otter goes unseen following a young otter family we'll reveal the secrets of the otter's world.

programme.title: Nuts about Squirrels
programme.desc: Squirrels have conquered tundra, deserts, forests and more, developing ingenious life skills along the way, from stashing nuts to fighting snakes.

programme.title: Mountains
programme.desc: Mountains define some of our most spectacular landscapes, creating a mosaic of dramatic habitats, teaming with incredible wildlife. Mountains are found on every continent on Earth, covering 22% of the planet's surface. They're a vital part of our ecosystem a quarter of terrestrial animal and plant species live in mountains and they house an incredible 85% of all amphibians, mammals and birds. It's no wonder that so many cultures consider mountains sacred places: gateways to the heavens.

programme.title: Fight for Territory
programme.desc: Biological instinct is driven by three things: the desire to stay alive, to find food and to breed. But to do all these things, animals first need a home. Out in the wilderness a safe place to call home can be the difference between life and death. A place to secure meals, raise their families and hide from danger. Ideal spots are in short supply and well worth fighting for! From lion scraps to insect warfare, these conflicts can provide us with the most remarkable spectacles in the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Colours of Nature
programme.desc: From subtle to shocking, colour enhances the beauty of the natural world. Colour is a language to most living things, whether as a warning, camouflage or a come on. But there's more to colour than just pigment. Colours define how many animals behave, how they mate and how they inter-act with one another. For us the beautiful and varied shades of the animal kingdom form a spec-tacular kaleidoscope of colours. With all the diversity in the world, sit back and enjoy all the col-ours of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Seasons Of The Otter
programme.desc: Most of the life of an otter goes unseen following a young otter family we'll reveal the secrets of the otter's world.

programme.title: Nuts about Squirrels
programme.desc: Squirrels have conquered tundra, deserts, forests and more, developing ingenious life skills along the way, from stashing nuts to fighting snakes.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: Rehabilitating and teaching young wild dog packs to hunt is what Margerita does at Erindi Private Game Reserve in Namibia. Margerita had discovered many of the wild dog packs in the region had succumb to illness and died. Margerita took it upon herself to raise an abandoned young pack of wild dogs. She teaches them to run as a pack, hunt as a pack and survive as a pack thus ensuring when the time comes the wild dog pack will survive in the wild. Alma's passion for yellow baboons in Malawi brought her to the African continent to live-out her passion. Her daily chores, activities and inspections keep her busy. Two yellow baboons meet each other for the first time when the gate separating them is opened. One of South Africa's smallest antelope known as a Cape Grys Bokkie loves playing hide ‘n seek it seems. When it comes to feeding the young antelope it always proves to be a challenge. However, this could be the nature of this timid little antelope.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: Lindi, the one-eyed Lion cub lives at Wildthingz Lodge situated in the northern region of South Africa. Having one eye does not restrict Lindi from been a playful and energetic lion cub. Skabenga, a young Hyena, also lives at Wildthingz. Skabenga has almost become part of the family, and his bond to his caretaker cannot be denied. Wolves in Africa? Tsitsikamma Wolf Sanctuary is an orphanage to abandoned wolves. Raising wolves on the other hand is not always an easy job. This is the case with an energetic seven-month-old wolf, Raul who knows no boundaries. We meet Victor Hugo at Somkhanda where the motto is to keep all the animals wild while rehabilitating them. We will meet a herd of Zebra and their young and discover that the dynamics in the family group are not always what they seem. Victor also rescues a family of owl hatchlings and ensures their safety by building a new house in a nearby tree.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: It's not surprising that raising the fastest animal on the planet involves regular exercise Frank Kasakamula at the Ranch Conservancy in South Africa has a number of strategies in place for the cheetah in his care. A traumatised, orphaned baby Vervet monkey is especially clingy and demanding. But from nappy rash to night feeds, Dean Oakes at the Bambelela Wildlife Sanctuary takes everything involved in being a ‘good mother' in his stride. Everyone at the Harnas Wildlife Foundation in Namibia has a right to life, including a blind, baby kudu named Derek. Soon his care taker, Louis Duvenhage, will introduce him to its new friends: a Springbok and a Duiker.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: In the Centre for Rehabilitation of Wildlife situated in KwaZulu Natal, South Africa, junior nurse Kylie is preparing grey duiker Major for his release into the wild. Major had suffered several injuries when he arrived at the centre, but with Kylie's help he recovered, and he will soon be back in his natural environment. We also travel to the Eastern Cape in South Africa to the main port city called, Port Elizabeth. Here Ruth, a dedicated animal caretaker, is constantly monitoring her loggerhead turtle hatchling Rocky, who has trouble with weight fluctuations. But once his nutrition is balanced enough that he can keep his weight for several weeks, he can be released into the Indian Ocean.Deep in the Cameroonian jungles we meet Rachel, a committed caretaker of primates at the Mefoue Primate Sanctuary. She arrived at the centre sixteen years ago and decided to stay after Khan Daniel, a new-born silverback gorilla, chose her as his surrogate mother. Since then her passion for these animals grew stronger from day to day. Sixteen years later Khan Daniel is the leader of the gorilla troop while Rachel now runs the private facility caring for these gentle giants of the forest.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: In this episode, we travel to Howick in Kwa-Zulu Natal, South Africa to a Centre called FreeMe Wildlife Rehabilitation. Here we meet Kirsten, also known as ‘Otter Mom number 1'. Kirsten is training her three orphaned Otters how to swim and hunt. The three mammals, Juliet, Oberon and Othelloe have almost completed their rehabilitation. After preparing the release site for two weeks it is time to return the three siblings back into the river. But persuading them to leave their temporary home might be more of a challenge than Kirsten anticipated. Situated at the base of the Hlumu Hlumu Mountains in the province of Mpumalanga in South Africa we find DIY Wildlife Rehabilitation. Animal caretaker Mitch oversees many different animals at his sanctuary. He is preparing to transport and release a troop of Mongooses onto a new farm. Mongooses play a vital role in the region by keeping the snake population in balance. And in the summer season there are lots of them. So mobilizing the family quickly and efficiently is the first priority. A one-hour drive east of Johannesburg, at Wildlife, Judy is rehabilitating a one month old Squirrel. This tiny rodent has been kept as a pet and was malnourished. Judy constantly has to monitor his weight. But the little animal seems to become stronger and more confident because is obviously doesn't want to be treated like a baby anymore. So Judy's efforts pay off in the end.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: At the Hoedspruit Endangered Species Centre in the province of Limpopo we meet a member of one of the most threatened species on the planet, a white rhino named Esme. Caretaker Natasha is looking after the nine-month-old and makes sure it can develop in a well-protected surrounding. And she has got some help from a rather unexpected companion for the little calf - a dog named David. Further north we visit Tanzania, where so-called “Hero Rats” are fulfilling special tasks. These giant pouched rats are able to detect landmines and sniff out diseases. One of these little heroes named Sykwalker is learning how to detect tuberculosis. Caretaker Estadiov provides us an insight into the training of these extraordinary animals. In our highlight story, we meet Jane and her orphaned bat Devan. Rewild in Limpopo is the only Centre in the area that cares for and rehabilitates bats. Devan is having trouble with an essential skill for every bat – flying or more specifically the take-off. Because once he is airborne he seems to master the art of flight effortlessly.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: An hour's drive from Johannesburg Warren is raising three cheetah cubs at the Lion and Cheetah Sanctuary. They are an endangered species – not only in South Africa but around the world. Warren is taking the three orphaned siblings for walks on a daily basis to give them the opportunity to experience being outside their enclosure. This is part of the preparation for their return to the wild. Convincing them to leave the enclosure is easy, trying to get them back in is a difficult task to undertake. In the South African Limpopo province, we meet clinic manager Nikita at the Moholoholo Animal Rehabilitation Centre. She's taking care of several different animals all of whom got their own sad story. One of them is a female honey badger who recently lost her cub post-partum. She is mothering a chain as a substitute for her cub so Nikita is trying to help her getting through this difficult phase. She is also monitoring vultures who have fallen victim to poisoning. And she introduces Havana to us - a juvenile caracal who was found abandoned in a hotel. She has obviously been raised by humans as she was found clinging to a milk bottle. In our highlight story we travel to Crag's View Wild Care Centre in Port Edwards along the south coast of Kwa-Zulu Natal. Here we meet Craig and a partially blind blue duiker. Craig didn't have high hopes for the antelope to survive, as she has been attacked by a large cat. But thanks to the passionate caretaker the bubbly animal is doing great today.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: At the Loebis Predator Park in Bela-Bella, a town in the South African Limpopo region, caretaker Annel is looking after orphaned animal babys. Part of her gang of little rascals are a wild dog puppy and a porcupine – both two months old – who have an unlikely friendship and team up against a greedy little predator who likes to steel their food – a same-aged lion cub. But in the end, all of them get along very well – even with the cheeky little mongooses. The highlight of our visit there is the birth of two new members of this unusual family. We also meet Deidre Joubert at the Wild and Free Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre in Mpumalanga who takes care of young barn owls and prepares a bunch of them for their return to the wild. The first step of this process is a release into a bigger enclosure, in which they will stay for seven days, before they finally get back to their natural environment. In our highlight story, we meet sister and brother team Chriszanne and Nicol Burger at the Felidae Wildlife Centre just outside a town called Kimberley in the South African province of Northern Cape. Their focus is on rescued wild cats of all sizes but they also take care for a family of bat-eared foxes – a very intelligent and brash species.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: In the Wokutakula Wildlife Rescue Centre located in Brits, 85 kilometres north of Johannesburg animal caretaker Juan is looking after a partially blind juvenile Tiger called Felix. Juan rescued the exotic animal just moments before it was going to be traded overseas. He tried to treat the tiger's eye condition but so far, his efforts were unsuccessful. There is no hope for Felix to be returned to the wild, but she has adapted to her new life – thanks to other Tigers, who accompany her and Juan, who sheperds her as good as he can. In the province of Limpopo, we meet Laita at the Moholoholo Animal Rehabilitation Centre. She takes care of two precious juveniles: a Black Leopard and a Serval. The Black Leopard was raised by humans and didn't get the right nutrition, so he was stunted in growth. Therefore, it wouldn't have any chance to survive in the wild. Laita oversees his rehabilitation and the young animal makes great strides. The Serval is the only survivor of four abandoned kittens that got rescued. He also comes along very well and is developing essential skills. For our highlight story we travel to the Mankwe Wildlife Reserve, where Dougal and Lynn are monitoring young and juvenile animals. Spotting giraffes and rhinos isn't easy but it's a rewarding task.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Baboon Mother: In South Africa, 76-yr. old Rita Miljo is raising the tiny baboon baby Rosie as if it were her own. As Rosie grows bigger and stronger, Rita takes her to a “baboon kindergarten”, where she is to form a new troop with others. We also witness Rita and her team releasing a troop of grown baboons into the wild. Wombat Baby Mater: Shaun Douglas of “Something Wild Wildlife Sanctuary” in Tasmania has become a surrogate “mom” to the tiny wombat Mater. As little wombats cling to their mothers for many months in their early lives, Shaun takes Mater with him wherever he goes – even to the shower and to bed!

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Orcas: The 2-yr. old orca girl Skyla it to become part of the orca show at the “Loro Parque” in Tenerife. Her nannies Mark Galán and Claudia Vollhardt think of many different games and costumes they can use to teach Skyla the different tricks. Will Skyla finally master the difficult “centerbelly”? Bat-Hospital: Xanadu, a one week old flying fox baby, enjoys the loving care of veterinarian Jeanny MacLean and animal carer Sandra Kühne. Also two new babies arrive at the busy Australian hospital: Paulie and Sophie have to say goodbye to their prior nanny Fay. Will they adapt to the hundreds of bats that live at the Jeanny's hospital?

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: White Tiger Pipo: Nanny Regina Hamza has been raising a white tiger cub named Pipo. As Pipo is now growing too strong and wild for Regina and her husband, he is to return to the Safari Zoo “Serengeti Park” and live with a white tiger dame named Bianca. But saying goodbye is not easy for Regina, and Pipo is not so sure how he will like his new home with Bianca… Seals: Francois Hugo is fighting for the lives of the seal population of South Africa – his unusual approach and love for these creatures has created a very close bond. He has adopted infant seals JT, Alpha and Amigo in his first attempt to raise three pups at once.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Elk Baby: Two week old elk baby Flori was left behind by his mother and now lives with four dogs and his foster father Harald - in his living room! Will he be able to reunite with his parents? Koala Robiana: 9 month old Koala Robiana was left without a mother and with many burns after a forest fire. Now nanny Lorraine takes loving care of the foundling. With the help koala expert Rolf Schlagloth, she hopes to get Robiana fit enough to return her to wild soon. Lion Babies: Dr. Michael Böer is the veterinarian at the “Serengeti Park” near Hannover. We join him on one of his check-up tours through the park to see how the animals and especially their babies are doing. Four new lion cubs have recently been born – a highlight even for the experienced wildlife doctor!

programme.title: Escape from Syria: Rania's odyssey
programme.desc: Rania Mustafa Ali, 20, filmed her journey from the ruins of Kobane in Syria to Austria. Her footage shows what many refugees face on their perilous journey to Europe.

programme.title: The climate science behind flooding | My animals burned alive' in Greece's wildfires
programme.desc: The climate science behind flooding: why is it getting worse? | My animals burned alive: the man putting his life back together after Greece's wildfires

programme.title: Climate carnage: whose job is it to save the planet?
programme.desc: In a crucial year for the climate, award-winning Guardian environment editor Fiona Harvey reflects on 30 years of Cops and meets the politicians, activists and scientists asking who is responsible for saving the planet.

programme.title: Puerto Rico after Hurricane Maria | The Yamuna, India's most polluted river
programme.desc: Puerto Rico after Hurricane Maria: 'We're American, too, why don't they help?' | The Yamuna, India's most polluted river

programme.desc: Black Pete: Why is the Dutch blackface tradition still going strong? | I don't want any more sadness in my life - comedy theatre about life in the Calais refugee camp

programme.title: The UK's first professional trans choir: 'It's a joyful act of resistance'
programme.desc: Anil Sebastian and Coda Galabov are the creators of a new choir called Trans Voices, which offers a space for trans and non-binary people who have felt unable to sing with their true voices in traditional choirs.

programme.title: Scars
programme.desc: Scars are not just skin deep - emotional and psychological histories lie beneath the healed wounds.

programme.title: The new green superpower? Oil giant Kazakhstan tries to wean itself off the black stuff
programme.desc: Kazakhstan is rich with oil, gas and coal but Nursultan Nazarbayev, its president for life, has committed the country to a dramatic shift from fossil fuels to green energy.

programme.title: The Apache war against copper mining: 'this is an act of desecration'
programme.desc: Chí'chil Biłdagoteel - otherwise known as Oak Flat, Arizona – is regarded as sacred ancestral land by the Apache people.

programme.title: Sprinter Factory

programme.title: Blood, sweat and fears: special report on abuse towards grassroots football referees
programme.desc: The FA has rebooted its Respect Campaign this season to protect grassroots referees in England but many continue to suffer both mental and physical abuse.

programme.title: United Voices
programme.desc: As work in the UK gets more precarious and the gig economy booms, trade unionism is being reimagined and people from a wide range of backgrounds are joining new kinds of unions, often for the first time.

programme.title: Funeral poverty: one woman's battle to pay for her son's burial
programme.desc: One suddenly bereaved mother, already in debt, has to find thousands of pounds to pay for her son's funeral.

programme.title: Frozen in Time: the motherhood dilemma for single women in China
programme.desc: Fertility tourism is booming for single Chinese women with hopes of future motherhood.

programme.title: Globalised slavery: how big supermarkets are selling prawns in supply chain fed by slave labour
programme.desc: A six-month Guardian multimedia investigation has, for the first time, tracked how some of the world's big-supermakets are using suppliers relying on slave labour to put cheap prawns on their shelves. Slavery is back and here's the proof. Narrated by Benedict Cumberbatch.

programme.sub-title: When the Berry Fairy Came to Stay

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors make an unusual type of cheese with Xand's body bacteria, find out what to do if someone breaks their leg in First Aid, and Dr Chris meets a man with a bionic eye.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors set fire to food [all in the name of science!] Dr Xand goes to an Asthma clinic, and in 'Ouch and About' the Ouch-Mobile opens its doors to solve more intriguing medical mysteries.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors get competitive in a race to reveal what happens when blood vessels get blocked, Dr Xand goes for some physiotherapy, and Dr Chris is out with the emergency services!

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors experiment on their muscles to show they work! Dr Chris meets some wriggly maggots that are helping to heal a sore foot and Dr Xand goes on-call with an emergency ambulance team.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors investigate how eating different foods can drastically affect you. Dr Xand donates his blood and finds out how it is processed to help save lives.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors explain what happens to your body when you feel travel sick, they investigate why it's important to get a good night's sleep and Dr Chris goes on call with an emergency ambulance team.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors explain how and why we breathe, Dr Chris takes a close look at snot and mucus and we find out all about the body's largest organ, the skin!

programme.title: Operation Ouch

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors show how your mouth, nose and stomach are all connected! Dr Xand enters a room colder than Antarctica and Dr Chris meets a robot surgeon.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors investigate how amazing your skin is at protecting your body, and they'll also show you a cool body trick to amaze your friends.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors show you what really happens when you sneeze! Dr Chris gets his hands on a real human brain and Dr Xand goes on call with an emergency ambulance team.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors see how much pain they can take in their laboratory, Dr Xand investigates how our shoes can dramatically change the way we walk and Dr Chris goes on call with an emergency ambulance team.

programme.title: The Book Of Total Badness
programme.desc: The NEXO-Knights must fight Jestro's Monsters of Destruction which are attacking all over the Kingdom. But Merlok smells a trap…is he right? In a dark, destroyed part of the kingdom, Jestro arrives at the ruins of a HUGE castle. The Book of Monsters knows this place: the great evil wizard Monstrux lived here. The Book sniffs out some powerful magic in the ruins: “The Book of Destruction.” With this, The Book tells Jestro they can destroy the NEXO-Knights and then attack the King's Castle and cause massive destruction. Jestro creates destructive monsters and sends them all over the realm, so the Knights must separate and fight single combats all over the place. The Knights lose these battles (lesson: they have to work as a team!) And, just when the Knights are scattered across the Kingdom, Jestro attacks the King's Castle! It looks like all is lost. When Aaron, who has escaped, returns to see Jestro's attack, he realizes he must gather his fellow Knights. Merlok says it's very dangerous, but Aaron says he must take the risk to gather the Knights and save Knighton.

programme.title: The Might And The Magic
programme.desc: Aaron races across the kingdom to gather the NEXO-Knights as Jestro and his Destructive Monsters attack the King's Castle. The Knights must get back to the capitol in time, or Jestro will rule the kingdom! Jestro, while still attacking the King's Castle, has second thoughts until the Book of Monsters orders the mad jester to feed him another magic book he's found (and has been saving.) It's the powerful “Book of Revenge!” Now, combining the powers of Revenge and Destruction, Jestro and his monsters will get their vengeance on King Halbert and the Kingdom of Knighton. Jestro and his monsters lay siege…will they destroy the castle?! Meanwhile, Aaron races across the Kingdom gathering the NEXO-Knights one at a time. As each Knight rejoins the group, they become a more and more powerful team. They even get Robin to join them as the Black Knight in his repaired power suit. Our Knights arrive, finally regrouped, fight off Jestro, save the castle and the King. It's a glorious victory! The King is so pleased after seeing how the brave NEXO-Knights saved his kingdom, he allows Macy to join them as an official NEXO-Knight.

programme.title: Back To School
programme.desc: When the NEXO-Knights are sloppy in a fight with Jestro, Merlok 2.0 sends them back to the Knights' Academy. Not only for a brush-up but to find out what Jestro is after in its hallowed halls. As they Knights battle Jestro – in a somewhat haphazard and lazy way – they discuss the fact that Jestro never wins and that they're always getting over on him. Despite defeating the jerky Jester and his evil Book, Merlok 2.0 discovers that something about the attack on the village is tied to the Knights' Academy. It looks like that's where they'll strike next. It wouldn't hurt for the Knights to go “back to school” to watch for Jestro and for some refresher training. Merlok is sure Jestro wants something at the Academy, but he doesn't know what it is. The Knights are very much “fish out of water” at the academy, even with the “cool kids” – Robin and Ava – showing them around. The next generation of Knights is being taught to integrate the NEXO-Magic of Merlok into their fighting techniques and these kids are good (so good, they initially kick the lazy, know-it-all Knight's butts!) Their old principal, the ever-ornery Headmaster Brickland, sets them straight: they're all still slackers, and they better get their acts together (especially Lance!) The Knights take shifts as “library helpers” much to the delight of the old squirebot librarian, MARGE. They're convinced Jestro is after a book…isn't he always after a book? When Jestro attacks, the Knights help organize the students and there's a big fight in the Academy Library versus Jestro and his Monsters. They drive Jestro off without letting him steal any books! Victory! But as Jestro leaves, we see him holding what the Book of Monsters really wanted: a snow globe with a castle in it that was sitting on the librarian's desk. In the Lava Lands, Jestro throws the snow globe into a volcano and it frees the massive Castle of Monstrux from its snowy magic prison

programme.title: Greed Is Good?
programme.desc: When Jestro uses the Book of Greed to rob Auremville of its riches, he also gets Lance's parents as prisoners and these demanding rich folks are too much for even monsters to handle. Jestro's not sure what to do with his new castle in the Lava Lands. “Every castle needs a big chamber filled with treasure” says the Book of Monsters so they've got to go out looting! The BOM convinces Jestro to call forth GENERAL MAGMAR, and Jestro appoints him as “general manager” of “castle operations,” much to Magmar's dismay. Meanwhile, Jestro and the BOM find the BOOK OF GREED. This will allow them to make greedy, rapacious monsters that will bring them all the treasure they want to fill their castle! They'll head to the richest town in the realm – AUREMVILLE! Meanwhile, in Auremville, the NEXO-Knights arrive for a huge “Gold is Good” ball at Lance's house. When the Monsters finally arrive in Auremville, a battle breaks out and the greedy monsters make off with everything…including Lance's parents, Goldie and Cuthbert Richmond who are clad in the finest golden garments and so look valuable! Lance will now be forced to live…poor. The Knights have to get his parents back. Meanwhile, his parents – Cuthbert and Goldie Richmond –think their imprisonment in Jestro's gold-filled castle is a “spa holiday” with the worst service. They order Magmar and the monsters around like servants and drive them all crazy. The Knights pile into the Fortrex and head for the Lava Lands, formulating a plan along the way to free Lance's parents. In a village half-way to Jestro's Castle, the Knights run into…Jestro and his monsters headed the other way with Lance's parents and all the gold they've stolen. There's a big fight, until they realize they might have the same goal and decide to deal. The BOM wants to give them back all the gold and Lance's parents. Lance says “No deal.” “We'll take the gold, you can keep my parents.” It's a weird and comical stand-off. Finally, Clay convinces Lance he has to take his parents back even if they don't always get along (and, it's the only way to get the gold back.)

programme.title: Peak Performance
programme.desc: The search for the Sandman leads Mateo and the others to the tallest mountain in the Dream World, but Lunia's hourglass puts a target on their backs.

programme.title: The Anomaly
programme.desc: A surprise visit from the Chief Inspector of the Night Bureau forces Mateo to hide Z-Blob, who goes on an adventure in the heart of Brooklyn.

programme.title: The Digiverse And Beyond, The Elemental Masters
programme.desc: Tackling a puzzle about technology in Ninjago. A riddle requires the Ninja to consider the powers and history of the Elemental Masters.

programme.title: The Jade Princess
programme.desc: In the wake of an assassination attempt targeting the royal family, the ninja invite the royal princess, HARUMI, into their makeshift family. Lloyd glimpses someone escaping over the rooftops with a sack on their back. Fearing it is the kidnapped princess, Lloyd takes up the chase! He discovers to his surprise, however, that the masked intruder is none other than the princess herself, who is not quite as docile or meek as she pretends. She has a habit of sneaking out of her chambers at night to feed the homeless who surround the Imperial Palace. This revelation only endears her to Lloyd more and Harumi and Lloyd quickly strike up a friendship. Unfortunately, trouble catches up to the ninja when the Sons Of Garmadon target the second Oni mask and attack the palace. The scope of the attack proves more than the Ninja were prepared for as the Sons of Garmadon detonate a series of subterranean bombs, bringing down part of the palace. Lloyd and Harumi escape onto the flying ship, DESTINY'S BOUNTY. But the victory is a pyrrhic one. The second Oni Mask has been stolen and Harumi's parents and Master Hutchins are feared lost.

programme.title: The Pranks
programme.desc: The friends are using their talents to pull a prank on Olivia. But who gets the last laugh? Stephanie finds the perfect front page story for the school paper! It's almost too good to be true. Emma and Andrea go undercover to solve the mystery of the two Jacobs.

programme.title: Andrea's Big Moment
programme.desc: When famous pop star Livi comes to Heartlake City to judge a talent show, our five friends Olivia, Mia, Andrea, Emma and Stephanie all decide to enter – and one of them, in particular, really wants to win but faces some serious challenge from an old rival. A talent show has come to Heartlake City and all the girls enter the talent competition hoping to impress the judges, especially their idol, pop star Chloe. No one wants a shot at stardom more than Andrea, which is why the other girls are surprised when she doesn't show up to practice before the first round of the competition. Very nearly eliminated prior to the big show, Andrea panics and throws herself into an intense practice-training mode, even shutting her friends out when they're trying to help her with her performance. Just moments before her live TV debut, Andrea realizes she's missing a key element to her act. Can she pull it off without her friends? And are the other girls willing to help after being pushed away?

programme.title: Kate's Island
programme.desc: The five friends leave for science camp that takes place on an island and during the trip, they meet the stand-offish Kate – but they soon realize that there's more to her than meets the eyes and that you shouldn't judge the book by its cover. No one is more excited than Olivia about science camp at a beautiful rocky offshore island with a lighthouse. But Olivia quickly gets off on the wrong foot with a mysterious girl named Kate who seems obsessed with a medallion necklace and the island's rumored history of pirates and treasure. Even worse for Olivia, Kate seems to be growing extra chummy with Mia, even sneaking around the island with her late at night. With the help of Stephanie, Emma and Andrea, Olivia is determined to get to the bottom of the girls' secret friendship and find out once and for all what they're searching for on the island, but they better not let Mrs. Stevens catch them out of their cabin past curfew.

programme.title: The Grand Hotel
programme.desc: The five friends help out at The Grand Hotel that Andrea's family just bought during what was supposed to be a quiet weekend until a hotel critic arrives unexpectedly. The girls are ready to do everything to make a great impression – but things don't quite go according to plan. Andrea, Stephanie, Olivia, Emma and Mia have come for breakfast at the fanciest hotel in town, now owned by Andrea's family. When Andrea's aunt has to leave town, the girls volunteer to run the hotel for the weekend with the help of quirky Grandma Jean. This is the perfect job for Stephanie's expert managerial skills but then the hotel suddenly fills to capacity with guests, including a hotel critic that can make or break the hotel's reputation! Now the girls find themselves in over their heads juggling reception, childcare, food prep and waitressing, a dog-walking service and a testy chef who quits on them in the middle of dinner. Unfortunately, much of this is their own fault. Can the girls save the Grand Hotel from themselves?

programme.title: Ninjaball Run
programme.desc: When the ninja learn that Darreth's Mojo Dojo is to be destroyed and Darreth doesn't have the money to save it, our heroes enter the annual NinjaBall Run to win its cash prize in order to save the dojo. As Lloyd continues to train and unlock his hidden powers, the Ninja learn land developers are going to destroy Darreth's Mojo Dojo unless Darreth can find the money to save it. The Ninja decide to enter their Ninja Tank into the annual NinjaBall Run cross-country rally to try to win its cash prize. Revisiting past characters who also enter the race, the high-speed derby will only grow more dangerous as Garmadon enters his Black Bounty, realizing this is the perfect chance to use his Mega-Weapon and rid the ninja. In the end, the Ninja will discover a few secrets within their Ninja Tank, allowing them to beat Garmadon's Bounty by a millisecond and saving Darreth's Dojo with their well-deserved cash prize. And as a final surprise, the Ninja steal back the Destiny's Bounty and make it their own once again.

programme.title: Child's Play
programme.desc: When the Ninja interrupt Lord Garmadon from using his Mega-Weapon to ‘turn back the clock' and bring to life the fossils of a monster, the Ninja are accidentally hit and turned into kids and are now forced to enlist Lloyd's help to battle the awakened monster. Tired of intense training, Lloyd yearns to play and enjoy his youth. The Ninja remind Lloyd that along with great power, comes great responsibility. When the Ninja interrupt Lord Garmadon from using his Mega-Weapon to ‘turn back the clock' and bring to life the fossils of a monster, the Ninja are accidentally hit and soon discover they've been turned into kids! Seeking Lloyd's help and relying on childhood antics, the five ninja kids must destroy the reawakened monster that's hunting them in the city. In the end, Lloyd makes the difficult decision and sacrifices his youth in order to save them from the beast, turning forward the clock and turning Lloyd into a young man. Sensei Wu fears now that since Lloyd is young man, the final battle of good versus evil approaches sooner than he had ever expected.

programme.title: The Surge
programme.desc: After a time of peace, the Ninja are called back into action. The spirit of the defeated Overlord has infected the electric grid of Ninjago and is using all available technology to exact his revenge on Lloyd. Nowhere is safe but fortunately, the Ninja have been given the powerful Techno Blades. But the only problem is that they have no idea how to use them. Time has passed since the Ultimate Battle, and while the Golden Ninja continues to watch over Ninjago, the other Ninja have become complacent as schoolteachers. When a mysterious invitation from genius Cyrus Borg brings them to the futuristic New Ninjago City, trouble surges when a digital virus of the Overlord takes over technology. The Golden Ninja makes a heroic entrance, only to find out his Golden Power supercharges the enemy. Sensei Wu stays behind so that the Ninja can escape with the only thing that can save Ninjago's future – Borg's Techno Weapons.

programme.title: The Art Of The Silent Fist
programme.desc: The Ninja go into hiding but an army of the Digtial Overlord's evil robotic Nindroids are hunting them wherever they go. Their only chance is to learn the secrets of the Techno blades and use them to cut all power to save Ninjao from this technological menace. The Ninja hide out at a safe place void of technology – Sensei Garmadon's Sanctuary. That night, Zane is visited by Borg's female robo-assistant, Pixal. She warns them that the Digital Overlord is after Lloyd's Golden Power in order to become flesh, but the Techno Blades can reboot the system and erase him for good. After a highly advanced army of Nindroids attacks the Sanctuary, Lloyd flees with his father into the wilderness of Ninjago, while the rest set off on a quest to turn off all power in Ninjago, so that they can erase the Digital Overlord once and for all.

programme.title: The Book Of Total Badness
programme.desc: The NEXO-Knights must fight Jestro's Monsters of Destruction which are attacking all over the Kingdom. But Merlok smells a trap…is he right? In a dark, destroyed part of the kingdom, Jestro arrives at the ruins of a HUGE castle. The Book of Monsters knows this place: the great evil wizard Monstrux lived here. The Book sniffs out some powerful magic in the ruins: “The Book of Destruction.” With this, The Book tells Jestro they can destroy the NEXO-Knights and then attack the King's Castle and cause massive destruction. Jestro creates destructive monsters and sends them all over the realm, so the Knights must separate and fight single combats all over the place. The Knights lose these battles (lesson: they have to work as a team!) And, just when the Knights are scattered across the Kingdom, Jestro attacks the King's Castle! It looks like all is lost. When Aaron, who has escaped, returns to see Jestro's attack, he realizes he must gather his fellow Knights. Merlok says it's very dangerous, but Aaron says he must take the risk to gather the Knights and save Knighton.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The disabling of Soundwave's deployer creates a unique opportunity for Ratchet and Wheeljack.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The final relic in the hunt may prove more deadly to Bulkhead than the Insecticon warriors who pursue him.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Spike is wounded and knocked unconscious in a Decepticon attack.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Wheeljacks' newest invention, The Immobilizer, can freeze any object in motion.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Optimus Prime sends the Dinogots to a remote and mysterious island.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Time portals are opening up all over the world.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Autobots battle against the Decepticons in a fate to save both Earth and Cybertron.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Sari Sumdac is coping with the newly discovered fact that she's a robot.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Things quickly descend from bad to worse for the Autobots when Detroit is menaced by new threats.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Optimus Prime prepares to return to Earth to face the mysterious Great Evil he's prophesied to Bumblebee.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Optimus Prime, Bee and his team must use everything they've learned together to stop Megatronus.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Autobots split into two teams.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: In a last-ditch effort, the Autobots and Decepticons work together to try and save their universe.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Starscreams fate is revealed a judge of the Quintessons and the interrogation of the Cybertronians begins.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Windblade battles her way into Iaconus.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Windblade wakes Iaconus only to discover the Quintessons have summoned their own titan.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Laconus falls and all looks lost until Megatron returns from the Multiverse with a new plan.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: In an attempt to find his missing friend, Bumblebee goes on a spy mission into Decepticon territory.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The only way to defeat Unicron is to wake the minicons inside him and attack from within as well as without.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Jokey's Shadow: Accidents do happen. This time it is Jokey Smurf's mistake that ends up with his shadow coming to independent life! And the shadow loves to play practical jokes on the whole population of the Village of the Smurfs...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Lazy's Slumber Party: Poor Lazy! All he wants is a good night's rest. But no, all the Smurfs throw a party for him and, what's more, he gets dragged into a silly argument about painting giant Bigmouth's toes a bright tangerine red... It's a long, sleepless night for poor Lazy Smurf!

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Puppy: Homnibus gives the Smurfs a dog that they name Puppy. The playful, normal-sized animal likes his new home, but Balthazar captures him, thinking that the Smurfs' magical secrets are in Puppy's collar. The Smurfs rescue the dog and Baby finds only music in the collar's locket.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Garagmel's Time Trip: Gargamel steals Father Time's hourglass in an effort to travel into the past and destroy the Smurfs, whom he blames for his failure in life. His plans are thwarted when Bigmouth shows up and breaks the spell, which only lasts if Gargamel is not seen by anyone.

programme.title: Secret Of The Village Well
programme.desc: Why is Clumsy Smurf waiting day and night near the Village well? What is the wish that he wants to be kept secret? How is Gargamel going to be mixed up in this mysterious affair? Is Gargamel going to catch the Smurfs at last?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Squeaky: This is the dramatic, moving story of the friendship between Smurfette and her pet mouse. A sad event reduces Smurfette to tears... Can the Smurfs comfort the despairing Smurfette?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Jokey's Funny Bone: Jealous of the attention Vanity Smurf receives when he breaks his toe, Jokey Smurf feigns sickness in hopes of getting recognition. Unfortunately, his ploy turns against him and instead of loving care all he receives is the proverbial pound of curse.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Littlest Viking: The Smurfs take Grandpa Smurf to the King's castle so that he can meet Peewit. But what with one thing and another (especially another), the Smurfs and Peewit are taken aboard a Viking long ship. The Vikings take Peewit to be their mythical Great Warrior, the one who is going to vanquish the Storm King. Can the Smurfs help Peewit out of his quandary?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Once In A Blue Moon: Once in a blue moon, as the legend goes, a baby Smurf is delivered to the Smurfs and everyone in the village becomes attached to the new arrival. But a mistake has been made and the stork returns to take the baby back. Grouchy and Smurfette have become so attached to the baby that the baby is returned.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Marco Smurf And The Pepper Pirates: The Smurfs sail off in their sailing boat to save Marco Smurf, who has been captured at sea by Captain Pepper. Marina and a dolphin help to free Marco.

programme.title: Brainy Smurf, Friend To All Animals
programme.desc: Brainy dons his safari hat and roams the forest to do good deeds for animals. He mostly gives advice to the creatures but balks when he can really save the life of a wildcat being hunted by Gargamel. Nat gives Brainy the courage to free the cat from Gargamel's trap.

programme.title: The Village Vandal
programme.desc: Nasty wizard Gargamel has had enough of the Smurfs' permanent superiority. He decides to destroy their Village once and for all. This is why he trains a termite to ravage, demolish, devastate and raze all the houses in its path. But the effect is even worse than Gargamel imagined as the Smurfs think that their Village is being torn apart by the antics of the Smurflings.

programme.title: Monster Smurfs
programme.desc: This is spook-a-Smurf eve, and all the Smurfs get ready for the fancy dress party… But Brainy Smurf gets hold of Papa Smurf's Book of Magic, and all the Smurfs are changed into hideous but funny creatures. Yet danger lurks...

programme.title: A Gift For Papa's Day
programme.desc: Gargamel steals Papa Smurf's birthday hat to lure the Smurfs into a trap. Papa tricks Gargamel into releasing them and then keeps the unusual hat to remind everyone of their great escape.

programme.title: Symbols Of Wisdom
programme.desc: Brainy Smurf desperately wants to be more like Papa Smurf. Can a hair tonic be the answer? Can Gargamel be able to finally capture the Smurfs? Does wisdom really reside in a beard?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Smurfs' Springtime: The wicked wizard Gargamel asks the sorcerer Balthazar for a recipe to capture the Smurfs. It involves kidnapping Mother Nature to stop the arrival of Spring. Luckily, the Smurfs save the day.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - A Loss Of Smurfs: When the emergency call rings, all the Smurfs in the Village drop what they are doing and run to hear the news. But when Vanity Smurf calls for an assembly so that the Smurfs can admire his new hat, it is too much. They send him on his way out of the village, and Vanity has the bad luck of meeting Lofty, the Elf, who suggests a horrible mud bath...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Greedy Goes On Strike: Get up early in the morning, before any other Smurf is out of bed, to prepare a scrumptious Smurfberry muffin. Spend all morning on preparing Smurf delicacies for lunch. Than all afternoon to have dinner ready – and delicious – on time. And do you get so much as a thank you form the Smurfs? So Greedy Smurf quits. The Village is in trouble... And soon, so is Greedy.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Sweepy Smurf: Sweepy the chimney sweep Smurf is not the most popular inhabitant of the Village of the Smurfs. He is rather messy, and the clouds of soot that he makes certainly don't add to his popularity. Only the Smurflings find him great company as he's the only one who can get dirty and not be told off. Yet in their enthusiasm for Sweepy, the Smurflings endanger the Village.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - It's A Puppy's Life: The evil wizard Gargamel has got hold of magical ectoplasmic crystal eggs that will enable him to change bodies with Papa Smurf. What better plan could there be? Disguised as Papa Smurf, he's going to lead all the Smurfs to his cauldron! But as fate would have it, Gargamel is changed into Puppy, the Smurf doggie...

programme.title: The Rabbids are having fun!

programme.title: The Rabbids are part of the family.

programme.title: The Rabbids Olympics.
programme.desc: Our two scientists put some Rabbids to the test to see if they have the right stuff for a trip to the moon…

programme.title: The Rabbids are too cold.
programme.desc: An old lady distracts a Rabbid, and he accidentally shuts himself inside a supermarket freezer.

programme.title: Rabbid Hibernation and the time machine.

programme.title: When I Was Your Age
programme.desc: Paul, 10, is a typical little boy. Except that as soon as someone tells him When I was your age, he is immediately hurled back to the time when the person he is talking to was 10 too.

programme.desc: Paul, 10, is a typical little boy. Except that as soon as someone tells him When I was your age, he is immediately hurled back to the time when the person he is talking to was 10 too.

programme.title: The Amazing Spiez
programme.desc: Megan is teased by her brothers because she is a poor skateboarder. Then they are called for a mission to investigate strange occurrences in Dubai, involving buildings and other structures breaking down. Later, Megan goes missing after getting her new skateboard that she will use to show her brothers that she can skate. Lee, Marc and Tony find her with an army of skateboarders responsible for breaking down the buildings so they can create skateparks for themselves.

programme.title: The Amazing Spiez
programme.desc: The Spiez are sent to protect a famous TV spy actor from various incidents. To keep an eye on him, the Spiez are invited to be in his Spy show, with the guidance of their parents who think they won a contest. The actor is kidnapped and the Spiez rescue him.

programme.title: Get Blake
programme.desc: Blake must master playing a wooden fish for a battle-of-the-bands performance with Skye. Followed by Sixty Second Dennis.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and his pet dog, Gnasher, live by just one rule: Kids should make all the rules!

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: The adventures of Dennis, his dog Gnasher, and their best friends JJ, Rubi and Pieface. The team cause havoc to Walter, Beanotown and Bash Street School, where all the rules go out the window.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher: Stunt Gran
programme.desc: Walter shows off some new tricks on his fancy skateboard, prompting Dennis to make a jibe that even his Gran could do tricks like that with such a high-tech boar and the bet is on! However, Walter secretly sabotages the trick, causing Gran to hang up her menacing shoes as a matter of public safety. It's up to Dennis, Gnasher and Angel Face to expose the truth and get Gran back to her wily self. Followed by more Dennis episodes.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Gnasher and Gnipper enter a dog show, regardless of Walter saying they don't have true talent. He wants Athena and Miss Mini Wuff to win, but Walter has just underestimated Gnasher's true capabilities. Followed by Grounded.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Athena gets Gnasher envy when seeing a pampered version of Gnasher in a magazine and when she hears that Dennis owns an Abyssinian wire-haired tripe hound (Gnasher), she wants him. However, being an owner to Gnasher isn't all cracked up to be! Followed by 'Splash'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis gets a case of the hiccups from Mr. Har Har's can-o-giggles and is looking for a way to cure them ready for a film premier. Followed by Athena's Party

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: The garden is accidentaly wrecked when Dennis tries to give Gnasher a bath, so the gang have to fix the mess before Mum comes home. Followed by 'Run Rabbit Run'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis makes a line of practical jokes for Mr. Scrimp, called 'The Menace Collection.' However, Dennis soon learns that it will put Mr. Har Har out of business.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After Walter takes up origami as a martial art, Dennis scoffs and comes up with his own martial art, and so the two prepare to battle it out. Followed by 'Smelling of Roses'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After watching a film about an alien invasion, Dennis, Curly and Pie Face decide to make their own film. However, Walter stumbles across them filming, and he only sees the action, not the camera. Filming what he sees on his mobile phone, he runs to tell the whole of Beanotown about the alien invasion. As panic spreads, Dennis and his friends discover the chaos and decide to keep filming in order to help make the best of their own alien film. Meanwhile, Sergeant Slipper investigates the alien landing that Walter has seen, and discovers the truth—that the spaceship is a playhouse with glow sticks in it. Followed by 'Castle in the Air'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and his pet dog, Gnasher, live by just one rule: Kids should make all the rules! Followed by Dance of the Seven Pies.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: To get his friends back from Walter's group, Dennis challenges Walter to a videogames battle. Followed by 'Fish Tale'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: For a school project, Dennis and Walter both build robots. However, during an accident, both robots merge, causing Dennis and Walter to defend the school. Followed by 'No Joke.'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: With one backstage pass for a Ratbucket gig, Dennis and his friends pass themselves off as Ratbucket, only for the real Ratbucket not to be able to play at his own concert. Followed by 60 second Dennis and 'Bad Dad'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After Dennis breaks his bike, Dad is unable to afford a new one, so Dennis convinces him to ask his boss for a raise. Unfortunately, Mr Scrimp reduces his wages instead. Dennis then trashes his house to convince Scrimp that his family are poor, so that Mr Scrimp will feel compelled to raise Dad's wages. Just as Scrimp has been taken in by Dennis' plan, Mum and Dad arrive home laden with shopping bags, resulting in Dad getting fired on the spot. Dennis resolves to get Dad his old job back by secretly causing chaos at his office. To restore order Scrimp rehires Dad, sealing the deal with a generous pay rise. Mum and Dad repair the house, but still don't have enough money to buy Dennis a new bike, instead it gets repaired with Dad's office rubber bands and paperclips. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Dennis Hiccups'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Believing Gnasher and Gnipper to be trapped in Beanotown Dog Pound, Dennis comes up with a plan to rescue his friends and bring them both home. Followed by more Dennis and Gnasher episodes.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: When Dennis breaks his skateboard again, Dad agrees to buy Dennis the best skateboard there is as a replacement if he is able to win a prize at the upcoming school award ceremony. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Dance of the Seven Pies'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis tries to get a birthday present by saying thank you to his relatives. However, because of Dennis' naughty personality, they take his attempts in a negative way. Followed by 'Dennis Hiccups'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: To get his friends back from Walter's group, Dennis challenges Walter to a videogames battle. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Masters of the House'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After a wrestling accident, Gnasher swallows a canister of laughing gas at the vet's. Dennis, along with Gnasher hide in the conference centre, where dad gives a speech. However, the canister leaks, causing Gnasher to fart laughing gas and having everyone laughing. Followed by 'Attack of the Walterbot'

programme.desc: Robotnik has his eyes set upon Queen Aleena's crown jewel, but Sonic, Manic and Sonia plan to retrieve the gem before Robotnik.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Tails gets a megadose of Robotniks growth gas and grows quickly to monstrous proportions, eating everything in sight and accidentally terrorizing the countryside.

programme.title: Beyblade

programme.desc: The BBA Revolution team fly to New York City for the Beyblade World Championships. The normally feisty Daichi proves to be afraid of flying, and once in the city, he becomes overwhelmed by the size and scope of things in the Big Apple.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: The teams have been selected and the finals are about to begin...Unfortunately Tyson finds himself facing all of his former friends...each part of a team that he will eventually have to 'blade against.

programme.desc: Tyson blames his embarrassing loss to Ray on Daichi, telling the team he's better off competing alone. As Tyson runs away, the US Finals continue with Rick of the PPB All Starz against Claude of Barthez Battalion.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: The Beyblade World Tag Team Championships are about to start in Rome as Barthez busily prepares his team for the big event. He's even invited a television news crew to document his efforts as a coach.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: Tyson gets in a huff and leaves the team after he is dropped from the lineup against the Blitzkrieg Boys. This leaves Kenny and Daichi to pick up the slack against their opponents.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: In the second of a best of three match, Tala thinks he's broken Kenny down, but Kenny summons his inner strength and attacks with all of his combined power. Unfortunately, it's not enough, and Tala shatters Kenny's Hopper into pieces with his Novae Rog attack.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: In Madrid for the Spanish leg of the World Championships, the hard-of-sleeping Lee has a nightmare that Ray kicks him off the team for his poor play. The day before their next match, White Tiger X encounters a pair of beyblading jugglers in Madrid's main square, who also happen to be trick bladers.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: As Max prepares to face Kai in the next round, the members of BBA Revolution overhear a confrontation between Johnny and Robert of the Majestics and Barthez Battalion.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: The BBA Revolution are slated to face the sneaky Barthez Battalion, but while Hilary and Kenny want to report their dirty deeds to the authorities, Tyson believes the best way to teach them a lesson is to beat them fair and square...But that's before he really sees how low the team will sink to win.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: Our heroes land in Egypt for the next leg of the tournament where the first two teams going up against each other are the Blitzkrieg Boys against the Barthez Battalion. But as Barthez readies his team, we learn he's devised a special spider web-like tape that is supposed to shoot out of his team's blades and trap their opponents in an inescapable web.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: Max is having trouble convincing Rick that he is truly dedicated to the PPB All Starz. Max just wants to play for love of the game but Rick thinks he should do anything to win. When the competition continues Max demands they fight a "pairs" match so that he and Rick are forced to work as a team.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: Tyson has just returned back from the World Championships with another victory and he's decided to teach a class of boys in the neighborhood the fine art of blading.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: Tyson has his hands full when he's confronted by a tough little Blader named Daichi. Just as they're about to battle another mysterious Blader named Jin shows up and wants to challenge Tyson as well. All three Bladers mix it up but Ray puts a stop to the fight when he realizes that Tyson can't handle two Bladers at once.

programme.title: Beyblade
programme.desc: Daichi continues to annoy Tyson with his constant battle challenges, which Tyson refuses to give in to - even after an embarrassing confrontation at the public swimming pool proves that Daichi is an impressive and highly skilled Beyblader.

programme.title: A Costume for Nicolas
programme.desc: Nicolás is a 10-year-old boy. Every year, his mother made him a disguise. Nicolás goes to live with Mia and Tomás, his loving grandparents, and his cousin David, who suffers from nightmares.

programme.title: Lassie: A New Adventure
programme.desc: The most famous dog in cinema history returns in a thrilling new family adventure!

programme.title: Bernard
programme.desc: S1:E1 The Gym | Golf | Ice Climbing | The Swing - Bernard is a hapless anthropomorphic polar bear who always tries to fix the troubles that regularly occur in each episode, but only succeeds in doing even worse damage, often even physically hurting himself. The character does not speak, but expresses himself with gestures and noises.
